initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% 2's in the same row left/right and same column up/down
left2(R, C) :- input(R, C1, 2), C1 < C, row(R).
right2(R, C) :- input(R, C2, 2), C2 > C, row(R).
up2(R, C)   :- input(R1, C, 2), R1 < R, col(C).
down2(R, C) :- input(R2, C, 2), R2 > R, col(C).

% A cell has a 2 somewhere in all four orthogonal directions
interior(R, C) :- left2(R, C), right2(R, C), up2(R, C), down2(R, C).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Interior cells must be colour3
:- interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% All noninterior cells must be colour0
:- not interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

#show output/3.

}

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% 2's in the same row left/right and same column up/down
left2(R, C) :- input(R, C1, 2), C1 < C, row(R), col(C).
right2(R, C) :- input(R, C2, 2), C2 > C, row(R), col(C).
up2(R, C) :- input(R1, C, 2), R1 < R, col(C), row(R).
down2(R, C) :- input(R2, C, 2), R2 > R, col(C), row(R).

% A cell has a 2 somewhere in all four orthogonal directions
interior(R, C) :- left2(R, C), right2(R, C), up2(R, C), down2(R, C).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Interior cells must be colour3
:- interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% All noninterior cells must be colour0
:- not interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% 2's in the same row left/right and same column up/down
left2(R, C) :- input(R, C1, 2), C1 < C, row(R), col(C).
right2(R, C) :- input(R, C2, 2), C2 > C, row(R), col(C).
up2(R, C) :- input(R1, C, 2), R1 < R, col(C), row(R).
down2(R, C) :- input(R2, C, 2), R2 > R, col(C), row(R).

% A cell has a 2 somewhere in all four orthogonal directions
interior(R, C) :- left2(R, C), right2(R, C), up2(R, C), down2(R, C).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Interior cells must be colour3
:- interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% All noninterior cells must be colour0
:- not interior(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

#show output/3.
